heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: 34.3mm per hour is the maximum rain rate ever recorded on 02 September 2025, during a 15.0 hour storm which dumped 21.19mm of rain from the clouds.

longest_rain.status: The lengthiest precipitation event recorded spanned 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025, it was characterized by a peak rain intensity of 24.6mm per hour and a total rainfall of 47.89mm.

most_lightning.status: The lightning strike count peaked at 23 on 07 September 2025, the highest since recording started on 11 April 2025.

storm.status: There is a storm in the vicinity.  This is the first storm since 21 December 2025.

top_10_dry.status: It is currently one of the longest dry periods.  It is the ranked 7th since our records started in 23 September 2024.  There have been 9 consecutive days where it has been dry starting on 23 December 2025.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 26 November 2025 and lasted for 14 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is not an active cold snap.  There has been 6 so far this year, while the average is 3.7.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 26.7°C (0.4 - 27.1°C), set on 29 April 2025.

heatwaves.status: There is not a heatwaves right now.  There have been 6 this year, compared to a usual average of 1.9.

tropical.status: The last tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and it has only happened 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is southerly (198°).  In the UK, a southerly wind usually brings milder, moist air from the Atlantic.

gusts.status: The highest wind gust ever seen is 93.7 mph on 13 October 2012.

run.status: To date, the maximum wind run recorded is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, cooler for the inside temperature (down 9.7°C), outside temperature (down 6.0°C), and feels like temperature (down 5.2°C).

Compared to today, yesterday also had noticeable differences in cloud levels (up 290 m).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside temperature (up 9.9°C), cloud levels (up 346 m), inside temperature (up 8.7°C), and inside humidity (up 7%) when compared to today.

cloud levels.description:
Today’s average cloud levels is 289.7m lower than yesterday, 346.4m lower than the past week, 28.8m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels for yesterday ranged from 828m to 2280m and averaged 991.2m, while the past week and month indicated warmer conditions.
cloud levels.summary: Cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times.

summary.summary: Inside; prepare for cold weather. Outside; icy, freezing temperatures. Also, the atmosphere will feel parched and dry and very little to no wind will be present. Possible light showers, cloud cover will be variable, overcast at times, and some sunny spells will occur. High pressure suggests settled weather.

washing.phrase: The current temperature is too low (1.6°C) for optimal drying conditions.  Any drying will take longer.
